topic: Too much self-reflection is linked to anxiety and depression.

framework.structure: ['The reason you'] + [Universal Relatable Behaviour] + ['is not'] + [Assumed Reason] + ['it is'] + [Dark Psychological Mechanism]
framework.trigger: Self-revelation — reframes a familiar experience with a disturbing explanation the viewer cannot unsee
framework.example: The reason you can't stop scrolling is not boredom. It is a dopamine loop engineered by behavioural scientists.

script.hook: The reason you can't stop analyzing yourself is not self-improvement—it's a trauma loop your brain won't release.
script.body: Every time you replay a conversation or dissect your flaws, you're reinforcing the neural pathway that says you're broken. Your brain treats self-criticism like a threat response, flooding cortisol to 'solve' the problem. But there is no problem to solve—only a cycle that feels productive while it destroys you.
script.cta: Drop a 👁️ if you've been stuck in this without knowing why.

full_script_text: The reason you can't stop analyzing yourself is not self-improvement—it's a trauma loop your brain won't release. Every time you replay a conversation or dissect your flaws, you're reinforcing the neural pathway that says you're broken. Your brain treats self-criticism like a threat response, flooding cortisol to 'solve' the problem. But there is no problem to solve—only a cycle that feels productive while it destroys you. Drop a 👁️ if you've been stuck in this without knowing why.

visual_direction.kling_prompt: extreme close-up eyes in deep shadow, single side light source, no full faces, 9:16 vertical cinematic, psychological thriller, deep shadows
